audit.message: High-dimensional small-n regime: 800 features vs 160 samples (p/n = 5.0). Univariate feature selection on the full dataset will find spurious signal; nested CV is mandatory.
Repeated measures present: 40 unique subjects for 160 rows (up to 4 rows/subject). A plain train/test split or StratifiedKFold puts the same subject on both sides. Use GroupKFold on subject ID.
Batch is confounded with the outcome (Cramer's V = 0.67, p = 1.4e-17). A model can score high by reading batch signatures in the features instead of biology. Correct for batch or split by batch.
Feature selection was performed on the FULL dataset before the train/test split. With p >> n this alone can manufacture a 0.9+ AUC from pure noise. Move selection inside the CV folds.
Scaling/imputation was fit on the full dataset (test rows informed the transform). Fit preprocessing on train folds only.
